name: readFile
description: Read the content of a text file. Path must be relative to scanned directory or absolute within allowed roots.
whenToUse: The user names a specific file path and wants its contents returned verbatim.
The path points to a single known file rather than a wildcard or pattern.
The user asks for metadata, text, or body of a previously-located file.
whenNotToUse: The user wants to discover files matching a pattern. Use glob instead.
The user wants to search inside many files for a regex or keyword. Use grep instead.
The user wants to move, rename, or otherwise revise the file. Use moveFile / renameFile / editFile instead.
